About: It is about an organisational principle that seeks unity in diversity.
It is about aiming to bring together anarchists of different tendencies into a single federation.

AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Nabat Confederation of Anarchist Organizations.

ConsistsOf: It consists of anarchists of different tendencies.
CreatedFor: It was created for anarchists of different tendencies.
CreatedTo: It was created to appeal to communists, syndicalists and individualists alike.
It was created to preserve the individual autonomy of its members.
It was created to foster cooperation between the various anarchist schools of thought.
It was created to unite anarchists across ideological lines.
It was created to unite anarchists into a single organisation.
It was created to encourage mutual aid between its members.
It was created to unite anarchists of different tendencies behind a set of common principles and goals.
It was created to form an organisational platform acceptable to the entire anarchist movement.
It was created to unify the disorganised anarchist movement into a coordinated revolutionary organisation.
It was created to combat the rise of Bolshevism.
DevelopedBy: It was developed by the Russian anarchist Volin.
It was developed by the French anarchist Sébastien Faure.
It was developed by Max Nettlau.
HasA: It has a principle of individual autonomy.
It has a loosely organised grouping.
It has a heterogeneous and relatively loosely organised structure.
It has a commitment to the principles of libertarian communism.

It is characterized by loose organisation.

It is characterized by ideological diversity.
It is characterized by mutual aid.
Influenced: It influenced the Nabat Confederation of Anarchist Organizations.
It influenced the formation of synthesis federations.
It influenced the International of Anarchist Federations.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the principle of "anarchism without adjectives".
It was influenced by the organisational ideas of Sébastien Faure.
It was influenced by the organisational ideas of Emma Goldman.
It was influenced by the Japanese "pure anarchists".
It was influenced by the model of the Nabat.
It was influenced by the Platform.
IsA: It is an organisational principle.
It is a federation.
It is a call for anarchists to unite into a single organisation.
It is a "synthesis" of the different tendencies.
It is a "united anarchism".
It is a coordinated revolutionary organisation.
It is a federation with its own unitary policy.
It is a conception of synthesis.
It is a federal association.
It is a synthesis federation.
KnownAs: It is known as the synthesis.
It is known as united anarchism.
KnownFor: It is known for seeking unity in diversity.
It is known for bringing together anarchists of different tendencies.
It is known for preserving individual autonomy.
It is known for encouraging mutual aid.

It occurred during the Ukrainian Revolution.
It occurred during the Russian and Ukrainian anarchist movements being forced into exile.
It occurred during the autumn of 1927.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the Soviet Union.
PartOf: It is part of the anarchist movement.
UsedFor: It is used for fostering cooperation between anarchist schools of thought.
It is used for uniting anarchists across ideological lines.
It is used for preserving individual autonomy.
It is used for encouraging mutual aid.
It is used for creating an organisational platform.
It is used for unifying the anarchist movement.
It is used for combating Bolshevism.
It is used for extending autonomy to each tendency.

It is used for leading an insurrection.
UsedIn: It is used in the anarchist movement.
